title: Japan crushes Tunisia 4-0 in the World Cup with two goals from Ueda
summary: The Japanese team achieved its biggest historical win in the tournament, defeating Tunisia with a resounding 4-0 that brings it closer to the round of 16.

title: Japan needs a draw against Sweden to advance to the round of 16
summary: The Japanese team will decide its qualification to the next round against a Swedish team that was recently routed by the Netherlands.

title: United States and Iran begin direct talks in Switzerland on nuclear program
summary: The talks, mediated by other countries, seek to reduce tensions in the Middle East, while clashes continue in Lebanon and the threat of a blockade in the Gulf.

title: Polls show majority support for government and opposition to Imperial House reform
summary: Surveys from four major media outlets show that 71% believe there is no urgency in adopting males from former imperial families, while cabinet approval hovers around 55%.

title: Fire at Tokyo elementary school started by an electric heater
summary: The fire in the fourth-floor music room did not cause injuries, but teachers could not use rescue equipment due to smoke.

title: Dismissed former mayor loses by 262 votes in Ishioka election
summary: The new mayor, Toida Kazuyuki, a former prefectural assembly member, won the election against the former mayor who had been removed by a no-confidence motion.

title: Actress Hoshino Mari to run '24 Hour TV' charity marathon
summary: The Japanese artist was selected as the runner for the charity event that aims to promote empathy in society.

title: Bullying at Tokaidai Osaka Kosei High School rugby club investigated as 'serious incident'
summary: The school, six-time national champion, faces bullying allegations that included the strangulation of a student.

title: Reports of British PM Starmer's resignation denied by government
summary: Although local media reported that he could resign on June 22, official sources assure he is focused on his work.

title: Japanese professional baseball results: Chunichi beats Yomiuri and Seibu beats SoftBank
summary: On the June 21 matchday, the Dragons overcame the Giants, while the Lions crushed the Hawks with a three-run homer by Watabe.

title: Alert level raised for Mount Aso volcano due to possible eruption
summary: The Meteorological Agency raised the warning to Level 2, restricting access to the crater due to the risk of falling rocks and pyroclastic flows.

title: Ukraine attacks refinery 2,000 km inside Russia with improved drones
summary: President Zelensky announced the attack on an oil facility in Russian territory, stating that domestic drones have been enhanced.

title: Basketball player Miyazawa Yuki reveals she has Graves' disease
summary: The Japanese player made her condition public to encourage others suffering from the same illness.

title: King Charles III and Prince William to publish their tax returns
summary: The British royal family seeks to increase transparency by making their tax contributions public.

title: Protest against hate speech gathers 650 people in front of Parliament
summary: Protesters defended the human rights of foreigners and rejected discrimination in Japan.

title: Over 2,100 people celebrate International Yoga Day at Tsukiji temple
summary: Participants practiced outdoor meditation at the event organized to commemorate the day.

title: Bolivia declares state of emergency due to protests blocking roads
summary: The Bolivian government took the measure in response to anti-government demonstrations that have caused unrest and road blockades.

title: China's rare earth magnet exports to Japan fall 34.6% in May
summary: The decline, exceeding the global average, could be linked to trade tensions between the two countries.

title: Chemistry Nobel laureate John Jumper leaves DeepMind for startup Anthropic
summary: The creator of AlphaFold joins the artificial intelligence team amid a growing labor movement in the tech sector.

title: Man dies hit by bullet train after accessing tracks through gap in fence
summary: The accident occurred at a station in Japan when the victim entered the railway area through a gap in the platform.
